behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names db.rs, TurboPersistence struct, is_empty(), load_directory(), commit(), AtomicBool, Ordering::Relaxed, and the active_write_operation field — all specifics tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially spells out the implementation: 'Add a new AtomicBool field', 'Rewrite is_empty() to use atomic .load() with Ordering::Relaxed', and 'call .store(meta_files.is_empty(), Ordering::Relaxed) at every location where meta_files is modified' — this is the fix, not just what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for an AtomicBool field (any name), .load( call in is_empty(), and >=2 .store(...meta_files.is_empty()...) calls — accepts reasonable variants.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests are static source-parsing via regex on db.rs; no code is compiled or executed (Dockerfile explicitly notes no Rust toolchain), so only text patterns are inspected.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require both removal of self.inner.read() and presence of .load(), plus two store() call sites in different functions — a stub returning constant is explicitly rejected.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the three instruction requirements: new AtomicBool field, lock-free is_empty, and sync at both mutation sites.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments ('Gates', 'Fail-to-pass') and descriptive docstrings.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction pinpoints exact file (db.rs), exact method to change (is_empty), exact API (AtomicBool, .load/.store with Ordering::Relaxed), and exact mutation functions (load_directory and commit) — fully localizes the fix.
